title: centaurea ispahanica subsp. macrocarpa
intro: Centaurea ispahanica subsp. macrocarpa, also known as the Ispahan cornflower, is a plant species belonging to the aster family (Asteraceae) and is native to the mountainous regions of Iran. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Centaurea ispahanica subsp. macrocarpa include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antioxidant and immunomodulatory effects. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
